objective: Architectural sign-off on the health-gated explore-mode surface for `grove serve` (GROVE-S02-T04).
key_changes: mcp.rs stays thin (surface selection + formatting); core::explore owns execution — respects the CLI/core boundary.
Startup gate D3: health_probe once pre-loop, surface bound for session; precedence force_standard > (force_explore|config) > health_probe is coherent.
Fail-safe: unhealthy/load-failure → transparent 7-tool fallback + stderr note; initialize always answers; stdout protocol-only.
Mid-session ProviderDown → isError:true with actionable message; server never crashes/exits.
AC-5 backward compat met: default mode byte-identical; Standard branch reuses tool_specs/instructions/call_tool verbatim.
explore tool schema is plain object (no anyOf/oneOf) — conforms to MCP checklist across all supported protocol versions.
Gates green: 178 tests pass, clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean, no 'fastcontext' string.
No cross-module impact; change confined to cli/src/main.rs and cli/src/mcp.rs.
Deployment: no version bump (sprint-final, additive), no regeneration, opt-in via config/flag.
Follow-up: add direct unit guards for missing-question isError branch and explore_tool_spec no-anyOf/oneOf shape.
